prompt:
Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. The reference image shows the locked vial geometry of a 5ml round BPC-157 vial, navy blue cap (hex #101850), studio lighting, label material, and bottom accent bar — reproduce all of these identically. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #101850. Vial size: 5ml, exact proportions matching the reference.

LABEL VERTICAL LAYOUT — IMPORTANT: drop the entire text block significantly lower than in the reference image. The TOP roughly 70 PERCENT of the label is a completely blank pristine white logo zone — generous empty white space, more than the reference shows. All text begins only in the LOWER 30 percent of the label. The product name 'BPC-157' must sit visibly BELOW the vertical midpoint of the label, not at it. The 'LOT: NH-1024' line should sit close to the bottom accent bar with only a small gap of white space below it.

LOCKED TYPEFACE FOR ALL TEXT: Helvetica Neue. Text content top to bottom: (1) Product name 'BPC-157' in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (~700) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered. (2) Directly below with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line 'xx mg · 5 ml' in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (same bold weight) at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, centered, single middle-dot separator. The 'xx' must be rendered LITERALLY as two lowercase letter x characters — intentional placeholder, not a number. (3) Below the dose with a small visual gap, two stacked compliance lines in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300, NOT bold), all-caps, medium grey, ~22 percent of the product-name size. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (4) Below with a small gap, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT, same small size, medium grey, perfectly horizontally centered. (5) At the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy accent bar at exactly hex #101850 must run UNINTERRUPTED EDGE-TO-EDGE across the full width of the label. Match the reference's hairline (if any) above the bar exactly. Absolutely NO additional hairlines anywhere.

Sharp focus, high resolution, clinical minimalist aesthetic. CRITICAL: render the dose line literally as 'xx mg · 5 ml' with two lowercase x's; do NOT replace 'xx' with a number. CRITICAL: text block must visibly sit lower on the label than in the reference — generous empty white space above 'BPC-157'.
